task_name: A.I. Simulator for Brain Surgery Training — News/Educational Feature
reason: This is instructional/news content, not an FLS task performance video
content_summary: This is a news feature about AI-assisted surgical simulators used for brain surgeon training at McGill University. The video contains interview segments with a news anchor and a medical expert, demonstrations of simulation software and hardware interfaces, and educational narrative about the use of AI to train neurosurgeons. There is no FLS (Fundamentals of Laparoscopic Surgery) task being performed.

video_segments_observed.content: News anchor introducing feature on A.I. Simulator helping brain surgeons practice
Close-up of person using laparoscopic simulator with brain tissue model on screen
Interview with medical expert in Tiverton, ON discussing AI simulation benefits
Demonstration of brain surgery simulator with 3D rendered anatomical model
Close-up of trainee hands operating laparoscopic graspers on simulator trainer box with tissue model

teaching_content.topic: AI-Assisted Surgical Simulation for Neurosurgery Training
demonstrated_techniques: 3D visualization of anatomical structures in virtual environment
Real-time haptic feedback during simulated procedures
teaching_content.educational_message: AI simulators enhance surgical training by providing realistic, repeatable practice scenarios for brain surgeons before operating on patients

confidence_rationale: Content clearly identified as news/educational feature with interviews, narration, and institutional branding. No FLS task rubric applies; video shows simulators and training context but not a scored trainee performance.
fls_scoring_not_applicable: This video does not contain a performance of an FLS task (Peg Transfer, Pattern Cut, Endoloop, Extracorporeal Suture, or Intracorporeal Suture). It is educational content about surgical simulation technology.
